name: ICD Coding — Saddle PE + Multi-Organ Failure + Psychiatric Comorbidities
description: Level 5 — MIMIC hadm 22205327 — 10+ ICD codes spanning cardiopulmonary, hepatic, renal, psychiatric systems
prompt: Patient: Female, 50 years old. Emergency admission. Presentation: sudden dyspnea and syncope. CT pulmonary angiography: saddle embolus of pulmonary artery with acute cor pulmonale. Thrombolysis with alteplase performed. Hospital course complications: acute and subacute hepatic failure with coma (ALT/AST >8000 IU/L), acute kidney failure with tubular necrosis (creatinine 3.5), other shock (BP 80/50), toxic encephalopathy, Clostridioides difficile enterocolitis, acute respiratory failure with hypoxia. Psychiatric history: anorexia nervosa restricting type, PTSD (unspecified), unspecified mood/affective disorder, other psychotic disorder, sleep terrors, borderline personality disorder, bipolar disorder (unspecified). Medical comorbidities: type 2 DM with polyneuropathy + foot ulcer (on insulin), GERD, COPD (unspecified), tobacco use, hyperlipidemia, hypothyroidism (other specified), hypertensive CKD, CKD unspecified, drug-induced nephropathy, hypokalemia, long QT syndrome. Labs: INR 3.0, PTT 65.4, fibrinogen 46, BUN 54, creatinine 3.5, bicarbonate 15, sodium 146, calcium 7.4. Assign the appropriate ICD-10-CM code(s) with correct sequencing.

codes.description: Saddle embolus of pulmonary artery with acute cor pulmonale
Acute and subacute hepatic failure with coma
Acute kidney failure with tubular necrosis
Type 2 diabetes mellitus with diabetic polyneuropathy
Hypertensive chronic kidney disease with stage 1-4 CKD
